diagrams.title: Randomized Quicksort Recursion Tree
diagrams.description: Shows how array is divided at each level with random pivot selection
elements: Level 1: two subarrays of sizes k and n-k-1 (random)
Worst case: n-1 and 0 (like sorted input with bad pivot)
Height: O(log n) average, O(n) worst
diagrams.purpose: Understand partition behavior and recursion depth

diagrams.title: Lomuto Partition Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of partitioning with last element as pivot
elements: For j from low to high-1: if arr[j] <= pivot: i++, swap arr[i],arr[j]
Result: elements < pivot on left, >= pivot on right
diagrams.purpose: Learn the partitioning mechanism step by step

diagrams.title: 3-Way Partition (Dutch National Flag)
diagrams.description: Visualization showing three-way split for handling duplicates efficiently
elements: Three regions: less than pivot (blue), equal to pivot (white), greater than pivot (red)
Three pointers: lt (less than), gt (greater than), i (current)
Invariant maintained: arr[0..lt-1] < pivot, arr[lt..i-1] = pivot, arr[gt+1..n-1] > pivot
Solves duplicate element problem in standard quicksort
diagrams.purpose: Understand efficient handling of duplicate elements
